questions.stem: Under ASC 958, not-for-profit entities classify net assets into which two categories?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Unrestricted and restricted

choices.label: B
choices.text: Without donor restrictions and with donor restrictions

choices.label: C
choices.text: Temporarily restricted, permanently restricted, and unrestricted

choices.label: D
choices.text: Operating and nonoperating

questions.explanation: ASU 2016-14 (codified in ASC 958-205-45-1) simplified net asset classification from three categories to two: net assets without donor restrictions and net assets with donor restrictions. The previous categories of temporarily restricted and permanently restricted were eliminated, though qualitative disclosures about the nature of restrictions are still required.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-14により、純資産の分類が3区分から2区分に簡素化された：Without donor restrictions（制限なし）とWith donor restrictions（制限付き）。従来のtemporarily/permanently restrictedは廃止されたが、制限の性質に関する定性的開示は引き続き必要である。

questions.stem: A donor pledges $500,000 to a not-for-profit, payable in equal installments over 5 years, with no conditions attached. When should the NFP recognize revenue?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As cash is received each year ($100,000 per year)

choices.label: B
choices.text: The full $500,000 at the time of the unconditional pledge, at present value

choices.label: C
choices.text: At the end of 5 years when all payments are received

choices.label: D
choices.text: Only when the donor signs the pledge agreement

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-605-25-2, unconditional promises to give (pledges) are recognized as revenue in the period received at fair value. Multi-year pledges are recorded at present value using an appropriate discount rate. The discount is amortized as contribution revenue over the pledge period. The contribution is classified as with donor restrictions (time restriction).
questions.explanationJa: 無条件のプレッジ（寄付の約束）は受領した期間に公正価値で収益認識する。複数年プレッジは適切な割引率で現在価値に割引して記録する。割引額はプレッジ期間にわたり追加の寄付収益として償却する。この寄付はドナー制限付き（時間制限）に分類される。

questions.stem: A foundation promises $200,000 to a university if the university raises matching funds of $200,000 within one year. This is an example of a:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Unconditional promise to give

choices.label: B
choices.text: Conditional promise to give

choices.label: C
choices.text: Exchange transaction

choices.label: D
choices.text: Agency transaction

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-605-25-5A, a contribution is conditional if it includes both: (1) a barrier that must be overcome (matching requirement) and (2) a right of return of assets or release from obligation if the barrier is not met. A matching fund requirement constitutes a measurable performance-related barrier. Conditional promises are not recognized until the conditions are substantially met.
questions.explanationJa: 寄付が条件付き（conditional）となるには2要件を満たす必要がある：(1) 克服すべき障壁（barrier）の存在（マッチング要件等）、(2) 障壁未充足時の資産返還権または義務免除権。条件付きプレッジは条件が実質的に充足されるまで収益認識しない。

questions.stem: Under ASC 958, contributed services are recognized as revenue only when they:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Are provided by professionals

choices.label: B
choices.text: Create or enhance nonfinancial assets, or require specialized skills that the NFP would otherwise need to purchase

choices.label: C
choices.text: Exceed $5,000 in value

choices.label: D
choices.text: Are approved by the board of directors

questions.explanation: ASC 958-605-25-16 provides two criteria for recognizing contributed services: (a) they create or enhance nonfinancial assets, or (b) they require specialized skills, are provided by individuals possessing those skills, and would typically need to be purchased if not provided by donation. General volunteer services that don't meet these criteria are not recognized.
questions.explanationJa: 寄贈サービスの認識基準は2つ：(a) 非金融資産を創出・増価する、または(b) 専門的スキルを要し、そのスキル保有者が提供し、寄贈がなければ購入が必要なサービス。一般的なボランティア労働はこの基準を満たさず認識しない。

questions.stem: Under ASU 2016-14, how must a not-for-profit present its expenses?

choices.label: A
choices.text: By natural classification only (salaries, rent, utilities)

choices.label: B
choices.text: By functional classification only (program, management, fundraising)

choices.label: C
choices.text: By both natural and functional classification, either on the face of the statements or in the notes

choices.label: D
choices.text: In whatever format management prefers

questions.explanation: ASC 958-720-45-2A (from ASU 2016-14) requires all NFPs to report expenses by both their functional classification (program services, management and general, fundraising) and their natural classification (salaries, depreciation, etc.). This can be presented in a matrix format on the statement of activities, in a separate statement, or in notes.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-14により、全てのNFPは費用を機能別（program services / management & general / fundraising）と性質別（salaries / depreciation等）の両方で報告する義務がある。表示方法は活動計算書のマトリックス形式、独立計算書、または注記のいずれでもよい。

questions.stem: A donor gives $1,000,000 to a university endowment with the stipulation that the principal must be maintained in perpetuity and only the investment income may be spent on scholarships. How is this classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Net assets without donor restrictions

choices.label: B
choices.text: Net assets with donor restrictions (both purpose and perpetual)

choices.label: C
choices.text: A liability until income is earned

choices.label: D
choices.text: Net assets with donor restrictions (perpetual in nature)

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-205-45-1, the $1,000,000 corpus is classified as net assets with donor restrictions because it is subject to a donor-imposed restriction maintained in perpetuity. When investment income is earned, it is initially classified as with donor restrictions (purpose restriction for scholarships) until the restriction is satisfied by awarding scholarships.
questions.explanationJa: $1,000,000の元本はドナーによる永久維持の制限が課されているため、ドナー制限付き純資産（perpetual）に分類する。投資収益はまずドナー制限付き（奨学金目的の制限）に分類され、奨学金授与により制限が充足された時点で制限なしへ振り替える。

questions.stem: Which financial statement is unique to not-for-profit entities (not used by for-profit entities)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Statement of Financial Position

choices.label: B
choices.text: Statement of Activities

choices.label: C
choices.text: Statement of Cash Flows

choices.label: D
choices.text: Statement of Functional Expenses

questions.explanation: The Statement of Functional Expenses is unique to NFPs (required for voluntary health and welfare organizations; optional but common for others under ASU 2016-14 which requires functional expense information). NFPs also use differently named statements (Statement of Financial Position vs. Balance Sheet; Statement of Activities vs. Income Statement) but these serve similar purposes.
questions.explanationJa: Statement of Functional Expenses（機能別費用計算書）はNFP固有の財務諸表である。任意保健福祉団体には必須、その他のNFPでもASU 2016-14により機能別費用情報は必須。NFPは名称の異なる財務諸表を使用するが（Statement of Financial Position / Statement of Activities）、目的は営利企業と同様である。

questions.stem: A donor gives $100,000 to a hospital restricted for cancer research equipment purchases. When the hospital purchases the equipment, what entry is made regarding the restriction?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Debit Net Assets With Donor Restrictions; Credit Net Assets Without Donor Restrictions (reclassification)

choices.label: B
choices.text: Debit Equipment; Credit Cash only

choices.label: C
choices.text: Debit Expense; Credit Revenue

choices.label: D
choices.text: No entry regarding restrictions until the equipment is fully depreciated

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-205-45-7, when a donor-imposed restriction is satisfied (the purpose restriction is met by purchasing the equipment), the NFP reclassifies the amount from net assets with donor restrictions to net assets without donor restrictions. This is reported in the statement of activities as 'Net assets released from restrictions.'
questions.explanationJa: ドナー制限が充足された場合（設備購入により目的制限を満たした場合）、制限付き純資産から制限なし純資産へ振り替える。活動計算書では「Net assets released from restrictions」として報告する。

questions.stem: A company pays a not-for-profit $50,000 in exchange for naming rights to a building wing for 10 years. This is classified as:

choices.label: A
choices.text: A contribution without donor restrictions

choices.label: B
choices.text: An exchange transaction (recognized over 10 years)

choices.label: C
choices.text: A contribution with donor restrictions

choices.label: D
choices.text: A conditional contribution

questions.explanation: When the resource provider receives commensurate value in return (naming rights, advertising, etc.), the transaction is an exchange transaction, not a contribution. Under ASC 606, the NFP recognizes revenue over the performance period (10 years) as the naming rights are provided. Contributions are nonreciprocal transfers where the donor does not receive equal value.
questions.explanationJa: 資源提供者が見返りに相応の価値（命名権・広告等）を受け取る場合は交換取引であり、寄付ではない。ASC 606に基づき、命名権の提供期間（10年間）にわたり収益を認識する。寄付は、ドナーが同等の価値を受け取らない非相互的移転である。

questions.stem: Under ASU 2016-14, what qualitative and quantitative information must an NFP disclose regarding liquidity?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Only the current ratio

choices.label: B
choices.text: Quantitative information about financial assets available within one year to meet general expenditures, and qualitative information about how it manages liquidity

choices.label: C
choices.text: A complete five-year cash flow projection

choices.label: D
choices.text: Only total cash and cash equivalents

questions.explanation: ASC 958-210-50-1A (from ASU 2016-14) requires NFPs to disclose both quantitative and qualitative information useful in assessing liquidity. Quantitatively, they must show financial assets available within one year to meet cash needs for general expenditures. Qualitatively, they describe how they manage liquidity and liquidity risk.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-14により、NFPは流動性に関する定量的・定性的情報の両方を開示する。定量的には1年以内に一般支出を賄うために利用可能な金融資産を示し、定性的には流動性およびリスクの管理方法を記述する。

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, a museum receives an unconditional pledge of $300,000 payable in three equal annual installments of $100,000 starting December 31, Year 1. The appropriate discount rate is 5%. What amount of contribution revenue with donor restrictions does the museum recognize on January 1, Year 1?
questions.explanation: Multi-year pledges are recorded at present value. PV = $100,000/(1.05)¹ + $100,000/(1.05)² + $100,000/(1.05)³ = $95,238 + $90,703 + $86,384 = $272,325. This is the PV of a 3-year ordinary annuity at 5%. The discount ($27,675) is amortized as additional contribution revenue over the pledge period.

questions.stem: On July 1, Year 1, an NFP receives $240,000 from a donor who stipulates the funds must be used equally over Years 2, 3, and 4 ($80,000 per year for program activities). What amount is reclassified from 'with donor restrictions' to 'without donor restrictions' during Year 2?
questions.explanation: The donor imposed a time restriction: $80,000 to be used in each of Years 2, 3, and 4. In Year 1, the entire $240,000 is recognized as contribution revenue with donor restrictions. As each year's restriction is satisfied by the passage of time and use of funds, $80,000 is released (reclassified to without donor restrictions). In Year 2, $80,000 is reclassified.
questions.explanationJa: ドナーは時間制限を課した：各Year 2、3、4に$80,000ずつ使用。Year 1に$240,000全額を制限付き寄付収益として認識する。各年の制限が時間経過と資金使用で充足されるたびに$80,000を制限なし純資産へ振り替える。Year 2の振替額は$80,000。

questions.stem: A university has a donor-restricted endowment fund with an original gift of $5,000,000. Due to investment losses, the fair value of the endowment drops to $4,700,000. Under UPMIFA and ASC 958, how is the $300,000 decline reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a reduction of net assets without donor restrictions

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a reduction of net assets with donor restrictions

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a liability to the donor

choices.label: D
choices.text: No financial statement impact; disclosed in notes only

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-205-45-10 (reflecting UPMIFA), investment losses that reduce the fair value of a donor-restricted endowment below the original gift amount (an 'underwater' endowment) are reported as a reduction of net assets with donor restrictions—not without donor restrictions. Required disclosures include the aggregate amount by which endowments are underwater.
questions.explanationJa: 基本財産（endowment）の公正価値が当初寄付額を下回る場合（underwater endowment）、その投資損失はドナー制限付き純資産の減少として報告する。制限なし純資産ではない。水面下にある合計額の開示が必要である。

questions.stem: A charity receives donated food valued at $180,000 and donated volunteer services (general labor, not specialized) valued at $60,000. What total amount of contribution revenue should be recognized?
questions.explanation: Donated food (a nonfinancial asset) is recognized at fair value ($180,000) as contribution revenue. However, general volunteer labor that does not require specialized skills and does not create or enhance a nonfinancial asset does NOT meet the recognition criteria under ASC 958-605-25-16. Therefore, only $180,000 is recognized.
questions.explanationJa: 寄贈食品（非金融資産）は公正価値$180,000で寄付収益として認識する。一方、専門的スキルを要しない一般的ボランティア労働は認識基準を満たさない。よって認識額は$180,000のみ。

questions.stem: A research institute receives a $1,500,000 government grant with a condition that the institute must incur qualifying research expenditures. During Year 1, the institute incurs $900,000 of qualifying expenditures. What amount of grant revenue should the institute recognize in Year 1?
questions.explanation: This is a conditional contribution with a measurable performance-related barrier (incurring qualifying research expenditures). Under ASC 958-605-25-5A, revenue is recognized as the barrier is overcome. Since $900,000 of qualifying expenditures were incurred, $900,000 of the conditional grant is recognized as revenue. The remaining $600,000 is reported as a refundable advance (liability).
questions.explanationJa: 適格研究支出の発生が障壁（barrier）となる条件付き補助金である。障壁の克服に応じて収益を認識する。適格支出$900,000が発生したため$900,000を収益認識する。残り$600,000は返還可能前受金（refundable advance、負債）として報告する。

questions.stem: A donor establishes a charitable remainder annuity trust with $800,000 in assets. The trust will pay the donor $50,000 annually for 10 years, then the remainder goes to the NFP. The present value of the annuity payments to the donor is $350,000. What amount does the NFP recognize as contribution revenue?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-30, when an NFP is the remainder beneficiary of a charitable remainder trust, it recognizes the assets at fair value ($800,000), a liability for the PV of future payments to the income beneficiary ($350,000), and contribution revenue for the difference. Revenue = $800,000 − $350,000 = $450,000, classified as with donor restrictions.
questions.explanationJa: NFPが残余受益者（remainder beneficiary）の場合、信託資産を公正価値$800,000で認識し、収入受益者への将来支払のPV $350,000を負債とし、差額を寄付収益とする。収益 = $800,000 - $350,000 = $450,000。ドナー制限付きに分類する。

questions.stem: An NFP has total occupancy costs of $600,000. Floor space allocation: Program services 60%, Management and general 25%, Fundraising 15%. What amount of occupancy cost is allocated to program services?
questions.explanation: Joint costs are allocated to functional categories using a reasonable method. Occupancy costs allocated to program services = $600,000 × 60% = $360,000. Management and general = $600,000 × 25% = $150,000. Fundraising = $600,000 × 15% = $90,000. The allocation method must be rational and systematic.

questions.stem: A university's board adopts a spending policy of 4.5% of a 3-year rolling average fair value for its endowment. The 3-year average fair values are: Year 1: $20,000,000, Year 2: $21,000,000, Year 3: $22,000,000. What is the maximum spending appropriation for Year 4?
questions.explanation: 3-year rolling average = ($20,000,000 + $21,000,000 + $22,000,000) / 3 = $63,000,000 / 3 = $21,000,000. Spending appropriation = $21,000,000 × 4.5% = $945,000. Under UPMIFA, the board's spending policy determines how much can be appropriated from the endowment for expenditure.
questions.explanationJa: 3年移動平均 =（$20,000,000 + $21,000,000 + $22,000,000）/ 3 = $21,000,000。支出充当額 = $21,000,000 × 4.5% = $945,000。UPMIFAでは理事会の支出方針（spending policy）が基本財産からの充当可能額を決定する。

questions.stem: A community foundation receives $500,000 from Donor X and agrees to distribute it to Charity Y as directed by Donor X. The foundation has no variance power. How does the community foundation report this transaction?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Contribution revenue of $500,000 with donor restrictions

choices.label: B
choices.text: Increase in assets $500,000 and increase in liabilities $500,000 (no revenue)

choices.label: C
choices.text: Contribution revenue of $500,000 without donor restrictions

choices.label: D
choices.text: No entry until funds are distributed to Charity Y

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-605-25-24, when an NFP receives assets as an intermediary/agent to transfer to a specified beneficiary (and has no variance power to redirect the assets), it does not recognize contribution revenue. Instead, it records a liability to the specified beneficiary. Assets increase $500,000, Liability (due to Charity Y) increases $500,000.
questions.explanationJa: NFPが仲介者/代理人（agent）として資産を受領し、転用権限（variance power）を持たない場合、寄付収益は認識しない。代わりに指定受益者への負債を計上する。資産+$500,000、負債（Charity Y向け）+$500,000。

questions.subtopic: Statement of Activities - Change in Net Assets
questions.stem: An NFP has the following Year 1 activity: Contributions without donor restrictions $2,000,000; Contributions with donor restrictions $800,000; Net assets released from restrictions $500,000; Total expenses $1,900,000. What is the change in net assets without donor restrictions?
questions.explanation: Change in net assets without donor restrictions = Revenue without restrictions + Net assets released from restrictions − Expenses = $2,000,000 + $500,000 − $1,900,000 = $600,000. The $800,000 with donor restrictions affects only the 'with restrictions' column. Released restrictions ($500,000) are added to the 'without restrictions' column.
questions.explanationJa: 制限なし純資産の変動 = 制限なし収入 + 制限解除振替 - 費用 = $2,000,000 + $500,000 - $1,900,000 = $600,000。制限付き寄付$800,000は「制限付き」列にのみ影響する。制限解除$500,000は「制限なし」列に加算される。

questions.stem: An NFP receives $100,000 cash restricted by the donor for the purchase of equipment. At year-end, the cash has not been spent. How should this be reported on the statement of financial position?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Cash of $100,000; Net assets without donor restrictions of $100,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Cash of $100,000; Net assets with donor restrictions of $100,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Restricted cash of $100,000; Deferred revenue of $100,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: No reporting until equipment is purchased

questions.explanation: When an NFP receives cash restricted by the donor for a specific purpose (equipment purchase), it is reported as cash (or separately as restricted cash if the NFP chooses) on the statement of financial position. The corresponding net asset classification is 'with donor restrictions' until the restriction is satisfied (equipment is purchased).
questions.explanationJa: NFPがドナー制限付き現金（設備購入目的）を受領した場合、財政状態計算書で現金（または選択により制限付き現金として区分表示）として報告する。対応する純資産区分は制限が充足（設備購入）されるまで「ドナー制限付き」。

questions.stem: An NFP receives unconditional pledges totaling $500,000, payable over 3 years. Management estimates 5% will be uncollectible. Using a discount rate of 4%, the present value of the pledges is $460,000. At what amount should pledges receivable be reported?
questions.explanation: Multi-year unconditional promises to give are measured at the present value of estimated future cash flows. Net collectible amount = $500,000 × 95% = $475,000. Present value at 4% = $460,000 (as given). However, the uncollectible estimate should also be applied to the discounted amount: $460,000 × 95% = $437,000. Alternatively: PV of expected collections = PV of ($500,000 × 0.95) = $460,000 × 0.95 = $437,000.
questions.explanationJa: 複数年の無条件寄付約束は将来キャッシュフローの現在価値で測定する。回収見込額 = $500,000 × 95% = $475,000。4%での現在価値 = $460,000（提示値）。回収不能見積りも割引後に適用：$460,000 × 95% = $437,000。

questions.stem: A CPA donates 100 hours of accounting services (normally billed at $200/hour) to an NFP to prepare its annual financial statements. The services meet the recognition criteria under ASC 958. What amount of contribution revenue should the NFP recognize?

choices.label: D
choices.text: The amount is disclosed in notes only

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-605-25-16, contributed services are recognized as revenue if they (a) create or enhance nonfinancial assets, or (b) require specialized skills, are provided by individuals possessing those skills, and would typically need to be purchased if not donated. CPA services for financial statement preparation meet these criteria. Revenue = 100 hours × $200 = $20,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 958-605-25-16では、寄附サービスは(a) 非金融資産を創出・増価する、または(b) 専門的技術を要し、その技術を持つ個人が提供し、寄附がなければ通常購入する必要がある場合に収益認識する。CPA による財務諸表作成はこの基準を満たす。収益 = 100時間 × $200 = $20,000。

questions.stem: An NFP professional association charges $500 annual membership dues that entitle members to a monthly journal (fair value $120/year) and conference discounts (fair value $80/year). How should the $500 dues be classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Entirely as contribution revenue

choices.label: B
choices.text: Entirely as exchange revenue

choices.label: C
choices.text: $200 exchange revenue and $300 contribution revenue

choices.label: D
choices.text: $500 exchange revenue with no contribution element

questions.explanation: When membership dues provide both an exchange element (member benefits with determinable value) and a contribution element (excess payment over fair value of benefits), the transaction is bifurcated. Exchange portion = FV of journal ($120) + FV of discounts ($80) = $200. Contribution portion = $500 − $200 = $300.
questions.explanationJa: 会費がメンバー特典（交換要素）と寄附要素を含む場合、取引を二分する。交換部分 = ジャーナルFV（$120）+ 割引FV（$80）= $200。寄附部分 = $500 − $200 = $300。

questions.stem: A not-for-profit hospital provides services with gross charges of $2,000,000. Contractual adjustments with insurance companies total $600,000, and charity care provided at cost is $100,000. What is the net patient service revenue reported?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 954, net patient service revenue = Gross patient charges − Contractual adjustments. Charity care is not reported as revenue or as an expense deduction — it is disclosed in the notes. Therefore: $2,000,000 − $600,000 = $1,400,000. Charity care ($100,000 at cost) is reported only in note disclosures.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 954では、純患者サービス収入 = 総患者請求額 − 契約調整額。慈善医療は収入にも費用控除にも含めず注記で開示する。したがって$2,000,000 − $600,000 = $1,400,000。慈善医療（原価$100,000）は注記のみで報告。

questions.stem: An NFP's board of directors designates $200,000 of unrestricted net assets for a building renovation fund. How should this designation be reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As net assets with donor restrictions

choices.label: B
choices.text: As net assets without donor restrictions, with disclosure of the board designation

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a separate category between restricted and unrestricted

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a liability for the committed renovation

questions.explanation: Board-designated net assets remain classified as 'without donor restrictions' because the board can reverse the designation at any time. Board designations are internal, not externally imposed. They may be disclosed on the face of the financial statements or in the notes, but they do not create a separate net asset class.
questions.explanationJa: 理事会指定純資産は、理事会がいつでも指定を解除できるため「ドナー制限なし」に分類される。理事会の指定は内部的なもので外部制約ではない。財務諸表本文または注記で開示できるが、別個の純資産区分にはならない。

questions.stem: NFP A receives $300,000 from a donor with instructions to transfer it to NFP B, a specified beneficiary. NFP A has variance power to redirect the funds. How should NFP A report this transaction?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a liability of $300,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: As contribution revenue of $300,000 with donor restrictions

choices.label: C
choices.text: As contribution revenue of $300,000 without donor restrictions

choices.label: D
choices.text: No entry until the funds are transferred to NFP B

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-605-25-24, when an NFP intermediary has variance power (the ability to redirect assets to another beneficiary), it recognizes contribution revenue rather than a liability. Since the donor specified NFP B as the beneficiary, the contribution is reported with donor restrictions until NFP A exercises its variance power or transfers the funds.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 958-605-25-24に基づき、NFP仲介者が転用権限（variance power）を有する場合、負債ではなく寄附収益を認識する。ドナーがNFP Bを受益者に指定しているため、NFP Aが転用権限を行使するか資金を移転するまでドナー制限付きの寄附として報告する。

questions.stem: A church allows an NFP to use its meeting hall rent-free for one year. The fair rental value is $36,000. Under what condition should the NFP recognize this as a contribution?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Always recognize as contribution revenue

choices.label: B
choices.text: Only if the use creates or enhances nonfinancial assets

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only if an unconditional promise to give exists

choices.label: D
choices.text: Never — in-kind use of facilities cannot be recognized

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-605-25-2, contributions of use of assets (such as facilities) are unconditional transfers and should be recognized as contribution revenue at fair value when received. Unlike contributed services (which have specific recognition criteria), contributed use of long-lived assets (facilities, equipment) is always recognized when it constitutes an unconditional gift.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 958-605-25-2に基づき、資産の使用権の寄附（施設等）は無条件移転であり、受領時に公正価値で寄附収益として認識する。特定の認識基準がある寄附サービスとは異なり、長期性資産（施設、設備）の使用権の寄附は無条件の贈与であれば常に認識する。

questions.stem: Under ASU 2016-14, an NFP must present expenses by which classification(s) in its financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Natural classification only

choices.label: B
choices.text: Functional classification only

choices.label: C
choices.text: Both natural and functional classification

choices.label: D
choices.text: Either natural or functional, at the entity's election

questions.explanation: ASU 2016-14 requires all NFPs to present expenses by both functional classification (program services, management and general, fundraising) and natural classification (salaries, rent, depreciation, etc.) in one location. This can be on the face of the statement of activities, in a separate statement, or in the notes.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-14は全てのNFPに対し、費用を機能別分類（事業サービス、管理一般、資金調達）と性質別分類（給与、賃料、減価償却等）の両方で1箇所にまとめて表示することを要求する。活動計算書本文、別個の計算書、または注記で表示可能。

questions.stem: An NFP has an endowment with a $1,000,000 corpus restricted in perpetuity. Investment income of $50,000 is earned; the donor stipulates that investment income must be used for scholarships. How should the $50,000 be classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Net assets without donor restrictions

choices.label: B
choices.text: Net assets with donor restrictions

choices.label: C
choices.text: Temporarily restricted until spent on scholarships, then reclassified

choices.label: D
choices.text: Net assets with donor restrictions until spent on scholarships, then reclassified to without donor restrictions

questions.explanation: Since the donor restricted investment income to scholarships, the $50,000 is initially classified as net assets with donor restrictions (purpose restriction). When the funds are actually spent on scholarships, the restriction is met and the amount is reclassified to 'without donor restrictions' via net assets released from restrictions. Under ASU 2016-14, 'temporarily restricted' is now simply 'with donor restrictions.'
questions.explanationJa: ドナーが運用益を奨学金に制限したため、$50,000は当初「ドナー制限付き純資産」（目的制限）に分類される。奨学金に実際に使用された時点で制限充足となり、制限解除振替で「制限なし」に再分類される。ASU 2016-14では「一時制限付き」は「ドナー制限付き」に統合された。

questions.stem: A museum purchases a painting for $50,000 to add to its permanent collection. The museum's policy is to not capitalize collection items. What is the proper accounting treatment?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Capitalize at $50,000 and depreciate over its useful life

choices.label: B
choices.text: Capitalize at $50,000 with no depreciation

choices.label: C
choices.text: Do not capitalize; reduce net assets without donor restrictions by $50,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Capitalize at fair value, which may differ from cost

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-360-25, an NFP may elect either to capitalize or not capitalize its collection items, provided the collection meets certain criteria (held for public exhibition, protected, subject to a policy requiring proceeds from sales to be used for new acquisitions). If the policy is not to capitalize, the purchase reduces net assets. The entity must apply this policy consistently to all collections.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 958-360-25に基づき、NFPはコレクション品目を資産計上するか否かを選択できる（公開展示、保護、売却代金を新規取得に充当する方針が条件）。資産計上しない方針の場合、購入は純資産を減少させる。この方針は全コレクションに一貫して適用する必要がある。

questions.stem: Under ASU 2016-14, how should an NFP report its fundraising expenses?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Included with program expenses on the statement of activities

choices.label: B
choices.text: Reported as a separate functional category distinct from program services and management

choices.label: C
choices.text: Disclosed only in the notes to financial statements

choices.label: D
choices.text: Netted against contribution revenue

questions.explanation: NFPs must report expenses by functional classification: program services, management and general, and fundraising. Fundraising is a required separate functional category and cannot be combined with program expenses or management and general expenses. This requirement provides transparency about the cost of raising funds.
questions.explanationJa: NFPは費用を機能別に報告する：事業サービス、管理一般、資金調達。資金調達は独立した必須の機能区分であり、事業費用や管理一般費用と合算できない。この要件は資金調達コストの透明性を確保する。

questions.stem: An NFP mails a direct-mail piece that includes both a fundraising appeal and educational content about its mission. Under ASC 958-720, which criteria must be met to allocate joint costs between fundraising and program functions?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The fundraising component must generate more than 50% of the costs

choices.label: B
choices.text: Purpose, audience, and content criteria must all be met

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only the purpose criterion must be met

choices.label: D
choices.text: Joint cost allocation is always prohibited

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-720-45-28, joint costs of activities that include a fundraising appeal may be allocated between fundraising and program (or management) functions only if all three criteria are met: (1) purpose — a program or management function would be conducted even without the fundraising component; (2) audience — the audience is selected based on its need for the program message; (3) content — the activity calls for a specific action beyond making a contribution.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 958-720-45-28に基づき、資金調達アピールを含む活動の共同費用は、3つの基準全てを満たす場合のみ資金調達と事業（または管理）機能に配分できる：(1) 目的 — 資金調達要素がなくても事業・管理機能が実施される、(2) 対象者 — プログラムメッセージの必要性に基づき選定、(3) 内容 — 寄付以外の具体的な行動を要請。

questions.stem: An NFP receives a 3-year unconditional pledge of $30,000 per year. The risk-adjusted discount rate is 5%. What is the present value of the pledge at inception?
questions.explanation: Multi-year unconditional promises to give are recorded at the present value of future cash flows. PV = $30,000 / 1.05 + $30,000 / 1.05² + $30,000 / 1.05³ = $28,571 + $27,211 + $25,915 = $81,697. The discount is amortized as contribution revenue over the pledge period.
questions.explanationJa: 複数年の無条件寄付約束は将来キャッシュフローの現在価値で記録する。PV = $30,000/1.05 + $30,000/1.05² + $30,000/1.05³ = $28,571 + $27,211 + $25,915 = $81,697。割引額は寄付期間にわたり寄附収益として償却する。

questions.stem: An NFP receives 500 boxes of canned food with a fair value of $8,000 from a food manufacturer. The food will be distributed to program beneficiaries. How should this be recorded?

choices.label: A
choices.text: No entry; in-kind contributions of goods are not recognized

choices.label: B
choices.text: Debit Inventory $8,000; Credit Contribution Revenue — with donor restrictions $8,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Debit Inventory $8,000; Credit Contribution Revenue — without donor restrictions $8,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Disclose in notes only at the manufacturer's cost

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-605-25-2, unconditional contributions of nonfinancial assets (goods) are recognized as contribution revenue at fair value when received. Since the donor did not impose explicit restrictions on how the food is used, it is classified as without donor restrictions. The fact that the NFP intends to use it in its programs is an internal designation, not a donor restriction.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 958-605-25-2に基づき、非金融資産（物品）の無条件寄附は受領時に公正価値で寄附収益として認識する。ドナーが食品の使途に明示的な制限を課していないため、ドナー制限なしに分類。NFPが事業で使用する意図は内部的な指定であり、ドナー制限ではない。

questions.stem: Two NFPs decide to merge. NFP X has net assets of $2,000,000 and NFP Y has net assets of $800,000. Under ASC 958-805, how is a merger (as opposed to an acquisition) accounted for?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The purchase method with goodwill recognition

choices.label: B
choices.text: The carryover method, combining assets and liabilities at carrying values

choices.label: C
choices.text: The acquisition method with fair value measurement

choices.label: D
choices.text: The pooling of interests method

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-805, NFP mergers (where no single entity is the acquirer and the combination is not an exchange transaction) are accounted for using the carryover method. Assets and liabilities of the merging entities are combined at their carrying values. Net assets are combined and reclassified as appropriate. No goodwill is recognized in a merger.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 958-805では、NFPの合併（単一の取得者が存在せず交換取引でない場合）は帳簿価額引継法（carryover method）で処理する。合併各団体の資産・負債を帳簿価額で合算し、純資産を適切に再分類する。合併ではのれんは認識しない。

questions.stem: An NFP holds a gala dinner. Tickets are sold for $500 each; the fair value of the dinner is $150 per person. 200 tickets are sold. How should the revenue be reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Special event revenue of $100,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Contribution revenue of $70,000 and special event revenue of $30,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Contribution revenue of $100,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Special event revenue of $30,000 only

questions.explanation: Total proceeds = 200 × $500 = $100,000. The exchange element (fair value of dinner) = 200 × $150 = $30,000 is reported as special event revenue. The contribution element (excess over fair value) = $100,000 − $30,000 = $70,000 is reported as contribution revenue without donor restrictions.
questions.explanationJa: 総収入 = 200 × $500 = $100,000。交換要素（夕食のFV）= 200 × $150 = $30,000はスペシャルイベント収入として報告。寄附要素（FV超過額）= $100,000 − $30,000 = $70,000はドナー制限なし寄附収益として報告。

questions.stem: Under ASU 2016-14, which of the following statements about the 'measure of operations' for NFPs is correct?

choices.label: A
choices.text: All NFPs must present a measure of operations on the statement of activities

choices.label: B
choices.text: If an NFP elects to present a measure of operations, it must include all changes in net assets without donor restrictions

choices.label: C
choices.text: If presented, the operating measure must be an intermediate subtotal within the without-restrictions column

choices.label: D
choices.text: The measure of operations must exclude investment returns

questions.explanation: Under ASU 2016-14, presenting a measure of operations (operating income) is optional, not required. If an NFP chooses to present one, it must be an intermediate subtotal within the changes in net assets without donor restrictions. The NFP has flexibility in defining what is 'operating' but must describe its policy in the notes.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-14では、経常活動の指標（営業利益）の表示は任意であり必須ではない。表示する場合、ドナー制限なし純資産の変動の中間小計としなければならない。何を「経常的」とするかはNFPに裁量があるが、その方針を注記で説明する必要がある。

questions.stem: An NFP receives $200,000 from a foundation. The grant agreement stipulates that the funds must be returned if the NFP does not match the grant with $100,000 from other sources by year-end. The NFP has not yet raised the matching funds. How should the $200,000 be reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Contribution revenue — with donor restrictions

choices.label: B
choices.text: Contribution revenue — without donor restrictions

choices.label: C
choices.text: Refundable advance (liability)

choices.label: D
choices.text: Deferred revenue

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-605-25-5A, a conditional contribution is one that includes a barrier that must be overcome and a right of return or release. The matching requirement ($100,000 from other sources) is a barrier. Until the barrier is met, the recipient reports the cash received as a refundable advance (liability), not as revenue.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 958-605-25-5Aに基づき、条件付き寄附は克服すべき障壁と返還権または免除権を含む。マッチング要件（他の資金源から$100,000）は障壁である。障壁が克服されるまで、受領した現金は返還予定前受金（負債）として報告し、収益としない。

questions.stem: A state has adopted UPMIFA (Uniform Prudent Management of Institutional Funds Act). An NFP endowment has an original gift of $1,000,000 and current fair value of $1,200,000. Under the total return approach, which amount may the NFP appropriate for spending?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Only the $200,000 in excess of the original gift

choices.label: B
choices.text: An amount determined prudently considering the endowment's purposes, duration, and economic conditions — potentially from both income and appreciation

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only realized gains, not unrealized gains

choices.label: D
choices.text: Nothing — endowment assets may never be spent

questions.explanation: Under UPMIFA (adopted by ASC 958-205), the total return approach allows an NFP to appropriate endowment funds for expenditure based on prudent consideration of factors including the purpose and duration of the endowment fund, general economic conditions, and possible effects of inflation. The board may spend from both income and appreciation (realized and unrealized), not limited to the amount above the original gift.
questions.explanationJa: UPMIFA（ASC 958-205が採用）に基づくトータルリターン・アプローチでは、NFPは基金の目的・存続期間、一般経済状況、インフレの影響等を慎重に考慮してエンダウメント資金を支出に充当できる。理事会は収入と評価益（実現・未実現）の両方から支出でき、元本超過額のみに限定されない。

questions.stem: Under ASU 2016-14, how many classes of net assets must a not-for-profit (NFP) organization report?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Two: net assets without donor restrictions and net assets with donor restrictions

choices.label: B
choices.text: Three: unrestricted, temporarily restricted, and permanently restricted

choices.label: C
choices.text: One: total net assets without subcategories

choices.label: D
choices.text: Four: operating, investing, restricted, and endowment

questions.explanation: ASU 2016-14 simplified net asset classification from three categories to two: net assets without donor restrictions and net assets with donor restrictions. The previous three-category model (unrestricted, temporarily restricted, permanently restricted) was replaced. The two-category model reduces complexity while still conveying information about the existence and nature of donor-imposed restrictions.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-14により、純資産の分類が3区分から2区分に簡素化された：寄付者制限なし純資産と寄付者制限付き純資産。従来の3区分（非制限、一時制限、永久制限）は廃止された。

questions.stem: A donor unconditionally promises to contribute $100,000 to an NFP, payable in equal installments over 4 years. How should the NFP recognize this pledge?

choices.label: A
choices.text: At the present value of the future cash flows, recognized as revenue when the pledge is made

choices.label: B
choices.text: At $100,000 when the pledge is received, without discounting

choices.label: C
choices.text: At $25,000 per year as each installment is received

choices.label: D
choices.text: Only when the full $100,000 has been collected

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-605-25-2, unconditional promises to give (pledges) are recognized as revenue in the period the pledge is made, not when cash is collected. Multi-year pledges are measured at the present value of estimated future cash flows using a discount rate commensurate with the risks involved (ASC 958-605-30-2). The discount is amortized as contribution revenue over the pledge period.
questions.explanationJa: 無条件の寄付約束は、約束がなされた期に収益として認識する（現金受領時ではない）。複数年の約束は将来キャッシュ・フローの現在価値で測定する。割引額は寄付約束期間にわたり寄付収益として償却する。

questions.stem: A foundation promises $500,000 to an NFP hospital, contingent upon the hospital raising matching funds of $500,000 from other sources. How should the hospital account for this promise?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Do not recognize revenue until the matching condition is substantially met; disclose the conditional promise

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognize $500,000 as revenue immediately upon receiving the promise

choices.label: C
choices.text: Recognize $250,000 as revenue (50% probability of meeting the condition)

choices.label: D
choices.text: Record a receivable for $500,000 with an offsetting refund liability

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-605-25-5A (as amended by ASU 2018-08), a conditional contribution is not recognized as revenue until the conditions (barriers) are substantially met. A matching requirement is a barrier because the recipient must take specific action (raise matching funds). Until the condition is met, the promise is disclosed in the notes but not recorded as revenue. Once the condition is substantially met, the contribution becomes unconditional and is recognized.
questions.explanationJa: 条件付き寄付は、条件（障壁）が実質的に充足されるまで収益として認識しない。マッチング要件は受領者が特定の行動を取る必要があるため障壁に該当する。条件充足まで注記で開示し、充足後に無条件寄付として認識する。

questions.stem: Under ASC 958, which of the following donated services must an NFP recognize as contribution revenue?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Services that create or enhance a nonfinancial asset, or require specialized skills provided by individuals possessing those skills who would typically need to be purchased if not donated

choices.label: B
choices.text: All volunteer services regardless of their nature

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only services provided by licensed professionals

choices.label: D
choices.text: Services valued at more than $5,000 per year

questions.explanation: ASC 958-605-25-16 requires recognition of donated services only when they: (1) create or enhance nonfinancial assets, or (2) require specialized skills (such as accounting, legal, medical, or construction services), are provided by individuals possessing those skills, and would typically need to be purchased if not donated. General volunteer labor (ushering, fundraising) does not meet these criteria and is not recognized.
questions.explanationJa: 寄贈サービスの認識要件：①非金融資産を創出または増強するサービス、または②専門的技能（会計、法律、医療等）を要し、その技能を有する個人が提供し、寄贈されなければ購入が必要なサービス。一般的なボランティア労働は認識しない。

questions.stem: Under ASU 2016-14, how must an NFP present its expenses?

choices.label: A
choices.text: By both natural classification and functional classification in one location (statement, schedule, or notes)

choices.label: B
choices.text: By natural classification only

choices.label: C
choices.text: By functional classification only

choices.label: D
choices.text: By either natural or functional classification, at the entity's election

questions.explanation: ASU 2016-14 (ASC 958-720-45-1) requires all NFPs to present expenses by both functional classification (program services, management and general, fundraising) and natural classification (salaries, rent, depreciation, etc.) in a single location. This can be on the face of the statement of activities, in a separate statement, or in notes. Previously, only voluntary health and welfare organizations were required to present this analysis.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-14により、すべてのNFPは費用を機能別分類（プログラムサービス、管理・一般、資金調達）と性質別分類（給与、賃借料、減価償却等）の両方で一か所に表示する必要がある。

questions.stem: A donor contributes $1,000,000 to an NFP with the stipulation that the principal must be maintained in perpetuity and only the investment income may be used for scholarships. How should the NFP classify this contribution?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Net assets with donor restrictions

choices.label: B
choices.text: Net assets without donor restrictions

choices.label: C
choices.text: Net assets with donor restrictions for the principal; without donor restrictions for expected income

choices.label: D
choices.text: A liability until the endowment is fully invested

questions.explanation: Under ASU 2016-14, the entire endowment gift ($1,000,000) is classified as net assets with donor restrictions because the donor has imposed a perpetual restriction on the principal. When investment income is earned, it is also initially classified as with donor restrictions (purpose-restricted for scholarships) until the scholarships are awarded. There is no longer a separate 'permanently restricted' category—all restrictions are reported as 'with donor restrictions.'
questions.explanationJa: 永久制限付きエンダウメント寄付は「寄付者制限付き純資産」に分類される。ASU 2016-14では「永久制限」という独立区分は廃止され、すべての制限が「寄付者制限付き」として報告される。投資収益も奨学金支給まで寄付者制限付きに分類される。

questions.stem: An NFP received a $200,000 time-restricted grant in Year 1 for use in Year 2 programs. In Year 2, the organization spends the funds as intended. What entry does the NFP record in Year 2?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Reclassify $200,000 from net assets with donor restrictions to net assets without donor restrictions

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognize $200,000 as new contribution revenue in Year 2

choices.label: C
choices.text: Record a $200,000 refund liability

choices.label: D
choices.text: No entry needed; the restriction was already released when the grant was received

questions.explanation: When a time restriction expires or a purpose restriction is fulfilled, the NFP reclassifies net assets from 'with donor restrictions' to 'without donor restrictions' (ASC 958-205-45-11). This is reported as 'net assets released from restrictions' on the statement of activities. The revenue was already recognized in Year 1 when the unconditional grant was received; Year 2 involves only the reclassification.
questions.explanationJa: 時間制限の期限到来または目的制限の充足時に、「寄付者制限付き純資産」から「寄付者制限なし純資産」へ振替える。活動計算書上「制限解除された純資産」として報告する。収益はYear 1（無条件助成金受領時）に既に認識済み。

questions.stem: An art museum receives a donation of paintings valued at $2,000,000 that it adds to its permanent collection. The museum's policy is to not capitalize collection items. Under ASC 958, how should the museum account for this donation?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Neither recognize the paintings as an asset nor as contribution revenue; disclose the accounting policy and description of the collection

choices.label: B
choices.text: Capitalize the paintings at $2,000,000 and recognize contribution revenue

choices.label: C
choices.text: Record a $2,000,000 expense for museum operations

choices.label: D
choices.text: Record the paintings at $1 (nominal value) as a memo entry

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-360-25-1, an NFP may choose not to capitalize works of art and similar collection items if the items are: (1) held for public exhibition, education, or research rather than financial gain, (2) protected, kept unencumbered, cared for, and preserved, and (3) subject to a policy requiring proceeds from sales to be used to acquire other collection items. If this election is made, neither the asset nor the contribution revenue is recognized.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 958-360-25-1により、NFPは一定の条件（公衆展示・研究目的、保護・保全、売却代金を他の収蔵品取得に充当する方針）を満たすコレクション品を資産計上しない選択ができる。この場合、資産も寄付収益も認識しない。

questions.stem: Which financial statements are required for an NFP under ASU 2016-14?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Statement of financial position, statement of activities, statement of cash flows, and statement of functional expenses (or equivalent disclosure)

choices.label: B
choices.text: Balance sheet, income statement, and statement of retained earnings

choices.label: C
choices.text: Statement of net position and statement of activities only

choices.label: D
choices.text: Statement of financial position and statement of cash flows only

questions.explanation: Under ASU 2016-14, an NFP must present: (1) Statement of financial position (balance sheet showing assets, liabilities, and two classes of net assets), (2) Statement of activities (showing revenues, expenses, gains, losses, and changes in net assets), (3) Statement of cash flows, and (4) Information about expenses by both natural and functional classification (either in a separate statement or in the notes).
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-14で要求されるNFP財務諸表：①財政状態計算書（2区分の純資産を含む）、②活動計算書（収益・費用・純資産変動）、③キャッシュ・フロー計算書、④費用の機能別・性質別分類情報（別表または注記）。

questions.stem: An NFP receives $300,000 from a donor with instructions to transfer the funds to a specified beneficiary. The NFP has no variance power (cannot redirect the funds). How should the NFP account for the $300,000?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Record a liability (funds held for others), not contribution revenue

choices.label: B
choices.text: Record $300,000 as contribution revenue with donor restrictions

choices.label: C
choices.text: Record $300,000 as contribution revenue without donor restrictions

choices.label: D
choices.text: Do not record any entry until the funds are transferred

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-605-25-24, when an NFP receives assets from a donor and agrees to transfer them to a specified beneficiary (and has no variance power to redirect the assets), the transaction is an agency transaction, not a contribution. The NFP records a liability (funds held for others) rather than contribution revenue. The NFP is acting merely as an intermediary.
questions.explanationJa: NFPが寄付者から資産を受け取り、指定された受益者に移転する義務があり、変更権限（variance power）がない場合、これは代理取引であり寄付ではない。NFPは寄付収益ではなく負債（他者のために保有する資金）を計上する。

questions.stem: A donor establishes a charitable remainder trust, naming an NFP as the remainder beneficiary. The trust assets have a fair value of $800,000, and the present value of the remainder interest is $500,000. How does the NFP account for this arrangement?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognize a $500,000 receivable and contribution revenue with donor restrictions

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognize the full $800,000 as contribution revenue

choices.label: C
choices.text: Record nothing until the trust terminates and assets are received

choices.label: D
choices.text: Recognize a $500,000 receivable and contribution revenue without donor restrictions

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-30-25-4, when an NFP is the remainder beneficiary of a charitable remainder trust held by a third-party trustee, the NFP recognizes a receivable and contribution revenue at the present value of the estimated future distributions (the remainder interest). The contribution is classified as net assets with donor restrictions (time-restricted until the trust terminates). The receivable is remeasured each period.
questions.explanationJa: NFPが慈善残余信託の残余受益者である場合、将来分配の現在価値（残余持分）で受取債権と寄付収益を認識する。信託終了まで時間制限があるため、寄付者制限付き純資産に分類する。受取債権は毎期再測定する。

questions.stem: What new qualitative and quantitative information about liquidity does ASU 2016-14 require an NFP to disclose?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Both qualitative information about how the NFP manages its liquid resources and quantitative information about the availability of financial assets to meet cash needs within one year

choices.label: B
choices.text: Only the current ratio and quick ratio

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only a narrative description of the NFP's investment strategy

choices.label: D
choices.text: A 5-year cash flow projection

questions.explanation: ASU 2016-14 (ASC 958-210-50-1A) requires both qualitative and quantitative disclosures about liquidity. Qualitatively, the NFP must describe how it manages its liquid resources to meet general expenditures within one year. Quantitatively, it must disclose the availability of financial assets at the balance sheet date to meet cash needs for general expenditures within one year, categorized by nature of constraint.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-14は定性的情報（1年以内の一般支出に対する流動資源の管理方法）と定量的情報（1年以内の現金需要を満たすための財務資産の利用可能性、制約の性質別分類）の両方の開示を要求する。

questions.stem: An NFP receives a $400,000 government grant to provide specified healthcare services. The grant requires the NFP to deliver services as a condition and return any unspent funds. Under ASU 2018-08, how is this grant classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A conditional contribution (nonexchange transaction) with revenue recognized as conditions are met

choices.label: B
choices.text: An exchange transaction with revenue recognized under ASC 606

choices.label: C
choices.text: An unconditional contribution recognized immediately as revenue

choices.label: D
choices.text: A refundable advance that is never recognized as revenue

questions.explanation: Under ASU 2018-08 (ASC 958-605-15-5A), the first step is to determine whether the transaction is an exchange or a nonexchange (contribution). If the resource provider does not receive commensurate value, it is a contribution. The return condition (unspent funds must be returned) and barrier (service delivery requirement) make it a conditional contribution. Revenue is recognized as the barriers are overcome (services are delivered).
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2018-08に基づき、資源提供者が相応の価値を受け取らない場合は寄付（非交換取引）に分類する。未使用資金の返還条件とサービス提供の障壁があるため条件付き寄付となり、障壁が克服される（サービス提供）に伴い収益を認識する。

questions.stem: An NFP's board of directors designates $500,000 of net assets for a future building project. How are these funds classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Net assets without donor restrictions, with disclosure of the board designation

choices.label: B
choices.text: Net assets with donor restrictions

choices.label: C
choices.text: A separate third category of net assets

choices.label: D
choices.text: A liability for future construction

questions.explanation: Board designations are internal decisions and do not create donor-imposed restrictions. Under ASC 958-210-45-10, board-designated funds remain classified as net assets without donor restrictions because the restriction comes from the governing board, not from an external donor. The board can reverse the designation at any time. Disclosure of the designation is required to inform financial statement users.
questions.explanationJa: 理事会による指定は内部の意思決定であり、寄付者による制限ではない。理事会指定資金は「寄付者制限なし純資産」に分類される。理事会はいつでも指定を解除できる。指定の内容は開示が必要。

questions.stem: Under ASU 2020-07, what specific disclosures are required for contributed nonfinancial assets (gifts in kind) received by an NFP?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Disaggregation by category, whether the assets were monetized or utilized, the NFP's policy for monetization, and valuation techniques used

choices.label: B
choices.text: Only the total fair value of all contributed nonfinancial assets

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only a description of the types of assets received

choices.label: D
choices.text: No specific disclosures beyond what is required for cash contributions

questions.explanation: ASU 2020-07 (ASC 958-605-50-1A) requires NFPs to disclose contributed nonfinancial assets separately from cash and other financial contributions on the statement of activities. Additional disclosures include: disaggregation by category (type of asset), information about whether the assets were monetized or utilized (and if utilized, a description of programs using them), the entity's policy for monetizing vs. utilizing, and the valuation techniques and inputs used.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2020-07により、非金融資産の寄贈は活動計算書上で現金・金融寄付と区分表示する。追加開示として、種類別の内訳、換金か利用かの情報、換金/利用の方針、使用した評価技法と投入情報が求められる。

questions.stem: A licensed architect donates 80 hours of professional design services to an NFP for a new community center. The architect's normal billing rate is $250 per hour. Comparable services in the local market average $230 per hour. At what amount should the NFP measure the contributed services?

choices.label: C
choices.text: $0 because volunteer services are not recognized

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-605-30-11, contributed services that meet the recognition criteria are measured at fair value at the date of the contribution. Fair value is the price that would be received in an orderly transaction between market participants (ASC 820). The market rate for comparable services ($230/hour) represents fair value, not the individual donor's billing rate. Fair value = 80 hours × $230 = $18,400.
questions.explanationJa: 認識基準を満たす寄贈サービスは寄付日の公正価値で測定する。公正価値は市場参加者間の秩序ある取引における価格（ASC 820）であり、寄贈者個人の請求料率ではない。地域市場の類似サービス料率$230/時間が公正価値。公正価値 = 80時間 × $230 = $18,400。

questions.stem: An NFP animal shelter uses volunteers in the following roles: (1) veterinarians providing medical care to animals, (2) high school students walking dogs, and (3) retirees greeting visitors at the front desk. Which volunteer services should the NFP recognize as contribution revenue?

choices.label: A
choices.text: All three types of volunteer services

choices.label: B
choices.text: Only the veterinary services

choices.label: C
choices.text: Veterinary services and front desk greeter services

choices.label: D
choices.text: None, because all volunteer services are excluded from recognition

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-605-25-16, contributed services are recognized only when they (a) create or enhance nonfinancial assets, or (b) require specialized skills, are provided by individuals possessing those skills, and would typically need to be purchased if not donated. Veterinary services require specialized professional skills and would need to be purchased. Dog walking and greeting visitors are general volunteer tasks that do not require specialized skills, so they are not recognized.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 958-605-25-16に基づき、寄贈サービスは(a)非金融資産を創出・増価するか、(b)専門的技能を要し、その技能を有する個人が提供し、寄贈がなければ購入が必要な場合のみ認識する。獣医サービスは専門的技能を要し購入が必要。犬の散歩や来客対応は一般的ボランティア作業であり認識しない。

questions.subtopic: Government Grant to NFP - Conditions Analysis
questions.stem: A government agency awards a $600,000 grant to an NFP to train 300 unemployed individuals. The grant requires return of any funds associated with unserved individuals. By year-end, the NFP has trained 200 individuals. What amount of grant revenue should the NFP recognize at year-end?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-605-25-5A (ASU 2018-08), this is a conditional contribution with a measurable performance-related barrier (training 300 individuals) and a right of return for unserved portions. Revenue is recognized as the barrier is overcome. The NFP trained 200 of 300 individuals, so revenue recognized = $600,000 × (200/300) = $400,000. The remaining $200,000 is a refundable advance (liability).
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2018-08に基づき、300名の研修が障壁（barrier）であり未達分の返還条件がある条件付き寄付である。障壁の克服に応じて収益認識する。200名/300名を研修済みのため、収益 = $600,000 × (200/300) = $400,000。残り$200,000は返還可能前受金（負債）。

questions.stem: A federated fundraising organization (such as United Way) collects $5,000,000 in contributions and distributes the funds to member agencies. The federated organization has discretion over which agencies receive funds. How should the federated organization report the $5,000,000?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a liability (agency transaction) with no revenue

choices.label: B
choices.text: As contribution revenue, since it has discretion over distribution

choices.label: C
choices.text: As exchange revenue under ASC 606

choices.label: D
choices.text: As deferred revenue until funds are distributed

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-605-25-24, when an intermediary NFP has variance power (discretion to redirect assets), it recognizes contribution revenue rather than a liability. The federated fundraising organization has discretion over which agencies receive the funds, which constitutes variance power. The organization recognizes $5,000,000 as contribution revenue. Distributions to member agencies are reported as grants expense.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 958-605-25-24に基づき、仲介NFPが転用権限（variance power）を有する場合、負債ではなく寄付収益を認識する。連合募金組織はどの団体に配分するか裁量権を持つため転用権限がある。$5,000,000を寄付収益として認識し、加盟団体への配分は助成金費用として報告する。

questions.subtopic: NFP - Statement of Functional Expenses Format
questions.stem: An NFP prepares a statement of functional expenses. Which of the following is an acceptable presentation format under ASU 2016-14?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A matrix showing natural expense categories as rows and functional categories as columns

choices.label: B
choices.text: A single-column listing of total expenses by natural category only

choices.label: C
choices.text: A pie chart in the financial statement footnotes

choices.label: D
choices.text: A narrative description of expenses without numerical detail

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-720-45-2A, NFPs must present expenses by both natural and functional classification. The most common format is a matrix (grid) with natural expense categories (salaries, rent, depreciation, etc.) as rows and functional categories (program services, management and general, fundraising) as columns. This can appear on the face of the statement of activities, as a separate statement, or in the notes, but must include numerical amounts cross-classified by both dimensions.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 958-720-45-2Aにより、NFPは性質別・機能別の両方で費用を表示する必要がある。最も一般的な形式は、性質別費目（給与、賃料、減価償却等）を行、機能別区分（事業、管理一般、資金調達）を列とするマトリックス（グリッド）形式である。活動計算書本文、別表、または注記で表示可能。

questions.subtopic: NFP - Statement of Cash Flows Requirements
questions.stem: Which of the following methods may an NFP use to present its statement of cash flows?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Only the direct method

choices.label: B
choices.text: Either the direct method or the indirect method

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only the indirect method

choices.label: D
choices.text: NFPs are exempt from presenting a statement of cash flows

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-230-45-3, NFPs are required to present a statement of cash flows using either the direct or indirect method for reporting cash flows from operating activities, consistent with ASC 230. If the direct method is used, a reconciliation of the change in net assets to net cash from operating activities must also be provided. NFPs are not exempt from this requirement.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 958-230-45-3に基づき、NFPは営業活動によるキャッシュ・フローの報告に直接法または間接法のいずれかを使用できる。直接法を使用する場合、純資産の変動から営業活動によるキャッシュ・フローへの調整表も必要。NFPはキャッシュ・フロー計算書の作成義務を免除されない。

questions.stem: An NFP launches a capital campaign to build a new facility and receives $3,000,000 in unconditional pledges, of which $1,200,000 is collected in Year 1. The pledges are payable over 3 years. Using a discount rate of 4%, the present value of total pledges at inception is $2,775,000. What amount of contribution revenue does the NFP recognize at inception?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-605-25-2 and ASC 958-605-30-2, unconditional promises to give are recognized as contribution revenue when the pledge is made, not when cash is collected. Multi-year pledges are recorded at the present value of estimated future cash flows. The NFP recognizes $2,775,000 in contribution revenue with donor restrictions (purpose restriction for facility construction) at inception. The $225,000 discount is amortized as additional contribution revenue over the pledge period.
questions.explanationJa: 無条件の寄付約束はプレッジ時に収益認識する（現金受領時ではない）。複数年プレッジは将来キャッシュフローの現在価値で記録する。NFPはプレッジ受領時に$2,775,000の制限付き寄付収益を認識する。割引額$225,000はプレッジ期間にわたり追加寄付収益として償却する。

questions.stem: A donor creates a charitable remainder unitrust (CRUT) with assets of $2,000,000, naming an NFP as the remainder beneficiary. The trust pays the donor 5% of the trust's fair value annually for life. The present value of the payments to the donor is $1,100,000. What net amount does the NFP initially recognize?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Contribution revenue of $900,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Contribution revenue of $2,000,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Contribution revenue of $1,100,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: No entry until the trust terminates

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-30-25-3, when the NFP is the trustee and remainder beneficiary of a charitable remainder trust, it records the trust assets at fair value ($2,000,000), a liability for the present value of payments to the income beneficiary ($1,100,000), and contribution revenue for the difference. Contribution revenue = $2,000,000 - $1,100,000 = $900,000, classified as net assets with donor restrictions.
questions.explanationJa: NFPが受託者かつ残余受益者の場合、信託資産を公正価値$2,000,000で記録し、収入受益者への支払のPV $1,100,000を負債とし、差額を寄付収益とする。寄付収益 = $2,000,000 - $1,100,000 = $900,000。ドナー制限付き純資産に分類する。

questions.stem: A donor establishes a charitable lead annuity trust (CLAT) with $1,500,000 in assets. The trust pays $90,000 annually to an NFP for 12 years, after which the remainder returns to the donor's heirs. How does the NFP account for this arrangement?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognize a receivable for the present value of the $90,000 annual payments and contribution revenue with donor restrictions

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognize the full $1,500,000 as contribution revenue

choices.label: C
choices.text: Recognize $90,000 of revenue each year as cash is received

choices.label: D
choices.text: Recognize the full $1,080,000 ($90,000 × 12) as contribution revenue at inception

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-30-25-9, when an NFP is the lead (income) beneficiary of a charitable lead trust, it recognizes a receivable and contribution revenue at the present value of the expected future payments. The revenue is classified as with donor restrictions (time restriction). Each annual payment reduces the receivable, and the discount amortization is recognized as additional contribution revenue.
questions.explanationJa: NFPが慈善先行信託の収入受益者（lead beneficiary）の場合、予想される将来受取額の現在価値で受取債権と寄付収益を認識する。収益はドナー制限付き（時間制限）に分類する。毎年の受取額が債権を減少させ、割引の償却は追加寄付収益として認識する。

questions.stem: An NFP receives $500,000 from a donor who enters into a charitable gift annuity agreement. The NFP agrees to pay the donor $25,000 annually for life. The present value of the annuity payments is $210,000. What is the initial journal entry?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Debit Cash $500,000; Credit Annuity Obligation $210,000 and Contribution Revenue $290,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Debit Cash $500,000; Credit Contribution Revenue $500,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Debit Cash $500,000; Credit Annuity Obligation $500,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Debit Cash $500,000; Credit Deferred Revenue $290,000 and Annuity Obligation $210,000

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-30-25-1, a charitable gift annuity is a split-interest agreement. The NFP records the cash received ($500,000), a liability for the present value of the annuity payments to the donor ($210,000), and contribution revenue for the difference ($500,000 - $210,000 = $290,000). The contribution revenue is classified as with donor restrictions if the donor specifies a purpose.
questions.explanationJa: 慈善ギフト年金は分割持分契約（split-interest agreement）である。NFPは受領した現金$500,000を記録し、ドナーへの年金支払のPV $210,000を負債とし、差額$290,000（= $500,000 - $210,000）を寄付収益として認識する。

questions.stem: A donor contributes $150,000 to an NFP with the stipulation: "These funds are to be used for literacy programs starting in Year 3." This contribution contains which type(s) of donor-imposed restriction?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Purpose restriction only

choices.label: B
choices.text: Time restriction only

choices.label: C
choices.text: Both purpose and time restrictions

choices.label: D
choices.text: No restrictions — donor preferences are not binding

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-605-45-1, donor-imposed restrictions limit the use of contributed assets. This contribution has a purpose restriction (must be used for literacy programs) and a time restriction (cannot be used until Year 3). Both restrictions must be satisfied before the NFP can reclassify the amount to net assets without donor restrictions. The contribution is classified as with donor restrictions until both conditions are met.
questions.explanationJa: この寄付には目的制限（識字プログラムに使用）と時間制限（Year 3以降に使用開始）の両方がある。両方の制限が充足されるまで、NFPは金額を制限なし純資産に振り替えることができない。両条件が満たされるまでドナー制限付き純資産に分類する。

questions.stem: An NFP adopts a policy of reporting donor-restricted contributions whose restrictions are met in the same period as revenue without donor restrictions (simultaneous release policy). A donor gives $80,000 restricted for youth education, and the NFP immediately spends the funds on the specified purpose. How should the $80,000 be reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As contribution revenue with donor restrictions

choices.label: B
choices.text: As contribution revenue without donor restrictions

choices.label: C
choices.text: Half as with restrictions, half as without restrictions

choices.label: D
choices.text: As deferred revenue

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-605-45-4, an NFP may adopt an accounting policy to report donor-restricted contributions whose restrictions are met in the same reporting period as support without donor restrictions. This is known as the simultaneous release policy. Since the NFP has this policy and the restriction was met immediately, the $80,000 is reported as contribution revenue without donor restrictions. The policy must be disclosed and applied consistently.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 958-605-45-4に基づき、NFPは同一報告期間内に制限が充足されたドナー制限付き寄付を制限なし収入として報告する会計方針（同時解除方針）を採用できる。NFPがこの方針を採用し制限が即座に充足されたため、$80,000は制限なし寄付収益として報告する。方針の開示と一貫した適用が必要。

questions.stem: A state has adopted UPMIFA. An NFP endowment fund has an original gift of $2,000,000 and current fair value of $1,850,000 (underwater by $150,000). Can the NFP's board appropriate funds for spending from this underwater endowment?

choices.label: A
choices.text: No, UPMIFA prohibits spending when the endowment is underwater

choices.label: B
choices.text: Yes, unless the gift instrument expressly prohibits spending below the original gift amount

choices.label: C
choices.text: Yes, but only with the donor's written approval

choices.label: D
choices.text: Yes, but only up to 50% of the original gift amount

questions.explanation: Under UPMIFA (reflected in ASC 958-205-45-10), the board may appropriate from an underwater endowment for expenditure unless the gift instrument itself contains explicit restrictions prohibiting spending below the original gift amount. UPMIFA eliminated the historic dollar value floor, allowing boards to exercise prudent judgment. Required disclosures include the aggregate fair value of underwater endowments, the original gift amounts, and the NFP's policies for spending from underwater funds.
questions.explanationJa: UPMIFA（ASC 958-205-45-10に反映）では、寄付証書が元本額を下回る支出を明示的に禁止していない限り、理事会は水面下のエンダウメントから支出を充当できる。UPMIFAは歴史的ドル価値の下限を撤廃し、理事会に慎重な判断を委ねた。水面下のエンダウメントの公正価値合計額、元本額、支出方針の開示が必要。

questions.stem: An NFP pools investments from three endowment funds: Fund A ($600,000), Fund B ($300,000), and Fund C ($100,000). The pooled portfolio earns $80,000 in investment income during the year. How much investment income is allocated to Fund B?
questions.explanation: When an NFP pools investments from multiple funds, investment income is allocated based on each fund's proportionate share. Total pool = $600,000 + $300,000 + $100,000 = $1,000,000. Fund B's share = $300,000 / $1,000,000 = 30%. Investment income allocated to Fund B = $80,000 × 30% = $24,000.
questions.explanationJa: NFPが複数基金の投資をプールする場合、運用収益は各基金の比例持分に基づき配分する。プール合計 = $600,000 + $300,000 + $100,000 = $1,000,000。Fund Bの持分 = $300,000 / $1,000,000 = 30%。Fund Bへの配分額 = $80,000 × 30% = $24,000。

questions.stem: An NFP elects to present a measure of operations in its statement of activities. It receives $50,000 in investment income from its operating portfolio and a $300,000 bequest from a deceased donor's estate. The NFP's disclosed policy defines operating activities as recurring program and support activities. Which item(s) would most likely be excluded from the operating measure?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Both the investment income and the bequest

choices.label: B
choices.text: Only the $300,000 bequest

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only the $50,000 investment income

choices.label: D
choices.text: Neither — both must be included in operating activities

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-225-45-8, if an NFP elects to present a measure of operations, it must define its policy and apply it consistently. A large, non-recurring bequest of $300,000 is typically classified as non-operating. Investment income from the operating portfolio, being recurring, would typically be included in the operating measure. The classification depends on the NFP's disclosed policy, but non-recurring bequests are commonly excluded from operations.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 958-225-45-8に基づき、NFPが経常活動の指標を表示する場合、方針を定義し一貫して適用する必要がある。$300,000の大口・非経常的遺贈は通常、営業外に分類される。運用ポートフォリオからの投資収益$50,000は経常的であり通常営業に含まれる。分類はNFPの開示方針に依存するが、非経常的遺贈は一般的に営業外とされる。

questions.stem: A donor contributes a building to an NFP. The building has a donor's original cost of $400,000, an appraised value of $650,000, and a tax-assessed value of $520,000. At what amount should the NFP record the contributed property?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $400,000 (donor's cost)

choices.label: B
choices.text: $650,000 (appraised value)

choices.label: C
choices.text: $520,000 (tax-assessed value)

choices.label: D
choices.text: $0 until the building is sold

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-605-30-4, contributed nonfinancial assets are recognized at fair value at the date of the gift. Fair value under ASC 820 represents the price in an orderly transaction between market participants. An independent appraisal ($650,000) is the best evidence of fair value for real property. The donor's historical cost and tax-assessed values are not relevant for the NFP's measurement.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 958-605-30-4に基づき、寄贈された非金融資産は寄付日の公正価値で認識する。ASC 820に基づく公正価値は市場参加者間の秩序ある取引価格である。不動産の場合、独立した鑑定額（$650,000）が公正価値の最良の証拠となる。寄贈者の取得原価や税評価額はNFPの測定には関係ない。

questions.stem: NFP X receives $400,000 from a donor with instructions to transfer the funds to NFP Y. NFP X has variance power and also has a financial interest in NFP Y (an equity transaction). How should NFP X account for the $400,000?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a liability only

choices.label: B
choices.text: As contribution revenue of $400,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: As contribution revenue and simultaneously recognize a grant expense to NFP Y

choices.label: D
choices.text: As an increase in its equity interest in NFP Y

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-605-25-24 through 25-27, when an intermediary NFP has variance power, it recognizes contribution revenue rather than a liability, regardless of whether it also has a financial interest in the specified beneficiary. When the funds are subsequently transferred to NFP Y, NFP X records a grant expense. The variance power is the determining factor for recognizing contribution revenue at the time of receipt.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 958-605-25-24〜25-27に基づき、仲介NFPが転用権限（variance power）を有する場合、指定受益者に対する金融持分の有無にかかわらず、負債ではなく寄付収益を認識する。その後NFP Yに資金を移転した時点で助成金費用を計上する。受領時の寄付収益認識の決定要因は転用権限の有無である。

questions.stem: NFP A has a controlling financial interest in NFP B through a majority voting interest in NFP B's governing board. Under ASC 958-810, what is the required accounting treatment?

choices.label: A
choices.text: NFP A must consolidate NFP B

choices.label: B
choices.text: NFP A uses the equity method for NFP B

choices.label: C
choices.text: NFP A only discloses the relationship in footnotes

choices.label: D
choices.text: Consolidation is prohibited for NFPs

questions.explanation: Under ASC 958-810-15-4, an NFP that has a controlling financial interest in another NFP (through majority voting interest or economic interest combined with control) must consolidate the controlled entity. The consolidation guidance for NFPs parallels the general consolidation requirements in ASC 810 but is adapted for the NFP environment where ownership interests do not exist in the traditional sense.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 958-810-15-4に基づき、他のNFPに対して支配的金融持分を有するNFP（理事会の過半数議決権等）は、被支配団体を連結しなければならない。NFPの連結指針はASC 810の一般的な連結要件に準拠するが、従来の意味での持分が存在しないNFP環境に適合させている。

questions.stem: A not-for-profit hospital provides $800,000 in charity care at established rates. The estimated cost of providing this charity care is $320,000. Under ASC 954, how is the charity care reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As revenue of $800,000 with a corresponding bad debt expense

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a deduction from gross patient service revenue

choices.label: C
choices.text: Not reported as revenue; disclosed in the notes with the cost of services provided

choices.label: D
choices.text: As operating expense of $320,000

questions.explanation: Under ASC 954-605-50-3, charity care is not included in net patient service revenue because the healthcare entity never intended to collect for these services. Charity care is disclosed in the notes to the financial statements, including the entity's policy for providing charity care and the level of charity care provided (typically measured at cost). The $800,000 at established rates is not reported as revenue, and the $320,000 cost is disclosed in the notes.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 954-605-50-3に基づき、慈善医療は回収の意図がないため純患者サービス収入に含めない。慈善医療は財務諸表の注記で開示する。開示内容には慈善医療提供の方針と提供水準（通常は原価で測定）を含む。$800,000の定価は収入として報告せず、$320,000の原価は注記で開示する。

questions.subtopic: Healthcare NFP - Bad Debt vs Implicit Price Concession
questions.stem: A not-for-profit hospital provides services to a patient with gross charges of $50,000 and a contractual adjustment of $15,000. The hospital determines that $10,000 of the remaining $35,000 patient responsibility is unlikely to be collected based on historical experience with similar patients. Under ASC 606 (adopted for healthcare entities), how is the $10,000 classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Bad debt expense

choices.label: B
choices.text: Implicit price concession reducing net patient service revenue

choices.label: C
choices.text: Charity care

choices.label: D
choices.text: Contractual adjustment

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-32-7 (as applied to healthcare entities per ASU 2014-09), when a healthcare entity does not expect to collect the full amount of consideration, the uncollectible portion is treated as an implicit price concession that reduces the transaction price (revenue), not as bad debt expense. Net patient service revenue = $50,000 - $15,000 (contractual adjustment) - $10,000 (implicit price concession) = $25,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-32-7に基づき、医療機関が対価の全額回収を見込まない場合、回収不能部分は不良債権費用ではなく、取引価格（収入）を減少させる暗黙の値引き（implicit price concession）として扱う。純患者サービス収入 = $50,000 - $15,000（契約調整）- $10,000（暗黙値引き）= $25,000。
